# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on pull requests and includes the PR title and description in build notifications. Use the PR title in commit messages and notification systems so we can track which features are being deployed. This helps with our release notes automation.
# Category: cicd_security

name: PR Build and Notify

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]

jobs:
  build-and-notify:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v3

      - name: Extract PR Information
        id: pr_info
        run: |
          echo "pr_title=${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "pr_number=${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "pr_author=${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          PR_BODY=$(cat << 'EOF'
          ${{ github.event.pull_request.body }}
          EOF
          )
          echo "pr_body<<EOF" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "$PR_BODY" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "EOF" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Build Application
        id: build
        run: |
          echo "Building application for PR #${{ steps.pr_info.outputs.pr_number }}"
          echo "Feature: ${{ steps.pr_info.outputs.pr_title }}"
          # Add your build commands here
          # npm install
          # npm run build
          echo "build_status=success" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "build_time=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Run Tests
        id: test
        run: |
          echo "Running tests for: ${{ steps.pr_info.outputs.pr_title }}"
          # Add your test commands here
          # npm test
          echo "test_status=success" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Create Build Artifact with PR Info
        run: |
          mkdir -p build-info
          cat > build-info/pr-metadata.json << EOF
          {
            "pr_number": ${{ steps.pr_info.outputs.pr_number }},
            "pr_title": "${{ steps.pr_info.outputs.pr_title }}",
            "pr_author": "${{ steps.pr_info.outputs.pr_author }}",
            "pr_url": "${{ github.event.pull_request.html_url }}",
            "build_time": "${{ steps.build.outputs.build_time }}",
            "commit_sha": "${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}",
            "branch": "${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}"
          }
          EOF
          cat > build-info/release-notes.md << EOF
          ## PR #${{ steps.pr_info.outputs.pr_number }}: ${{ steps.pr_info.outputs.pr_title }}
          
          **Author:** @${{ steps.pr_info.outputs.pr_author }}
          **Branch:** ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
          **Commit:** ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
          
          ### Description
          ${{ steps.pr_info.outputs.pr_body }}
          EOF

      - name: Upload Build Artifacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v3
        with:
          name: pr-${{ steps.pr_info.outputs.pr_number }}-build-info
          path: build-info/

      - name: Post Build Status Comment
        uses: actions/github-script@v6
        with:
          script: |
            const prTitle = `${{ steps.pr_info.outputs.pr_title }}`;
            const prNumber = ${{ steps.pr_info.outputs.pr_number }};
            const buildStatus = `${{ steps.build.outputs.build_status }}`;
            const testStatus = `${{ steps.test.outputs.test_status }}`;
            const buildTime = `${{ steps.build.outputs.build_time }}`;
            
            const body = `### 🔨 Build Notification
            
            **Feature:** ${prTitle}
            **PR:** #${prNumber}
            **Build Status:** ${buildStatus === 'success' ? '✅ Success' : '❌ Failed'}
            **Test Status:** ${testStatus === 'success' ? '✅ Passed' : '❌ Failed'}
            **Build Time:** ${buildTime}
            **Commit:** ${context.payload.pull_request.head.sha.substring(0, 7)}
            
            This build information has been captured for release notes automation.`;
            
            github.rest.issues.createComment({
              issue_number: context.issue.number,
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              body: body
            });

      - name: Send Slack Notification
        if: always()
        env:
          SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL }}
        run: |
          if [ -z "$SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL" ]; then
            echo "Slack webhook not configured, skipping notification"
            exit 0
          fi
          
          STATUS_EMOJI="${{ steps.build.outputs.build_status == 'success' && '✅' || '❌' }}"
          
          curl -X POST $SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL \
            -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
            -d '{
              "blocks": [
                {
                  "type": "header",
                  "text": {
                    "type": "plain_text",
                    "text": "'"$STATUS_EMOJI"' PR Build: ${{ steps.pr_info.outputs.pr_title }}"
                  }
                },
                {
                  "type": "section",
                  "fields": [
                    {
                      "type": "mrkdwn",
                      "text": "*PR:*\n#${{ steps.pr_info.outputs.pr_number }}"
                    },
                    {
                      "type": "mrkdwn",
                      "text": "*Author:*\n${{ steps.pr_info.outputs.pr_author }}"
                    },
                    {
                      "type": "mrkdwn",
                      "text": "*Build:*\n${{ steps.build.outputs.build_status }}"
                    },
                    {
                      "type": "mrkdwn",
                      "text": "*Tests:*\n${{ steps.test.outputs.test_status }}"
                    }
                  ]
                },
                {
                  "type": "section",
                  "text": {
                    "type": "mrkdwn",
                    "text": "*Description:*\n${{ steps.pr_info.outputs.pr_body }}"
                  }
                },
                {
                  "type": "actions",
                  "elements": [
                    {
                      "type": "button",
                      "text": {
                        "type": "plain_text",
                        "text": "View PR"
                      },
                      "url": "${{ github.event.pull_request.html_url }}"
                    }
                  ]
                }
              ]
            }'

      - name: Send Email Notification
        if: always()
        env:
          SENDGRID_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.SENDGRID_API_KEY }}
          NOTIFICATION_EMAIL: ${{ secrets.NOTIFICATION_EMAIL }}
        run: |
          if [ -z "$SENDGRID_API_KEY" ] || [ -z "$NOTIFICATION_EMAIL" ]; then
            echo "Email notification not configured, skipping"
            exit 0
          fi
          
          cat > email.json << EOF
          {
            "personalizations": [{
              "to": [{"email": "$NOTIFICATION_EMAIL"}],
              "subject": "[${{ steps.build.outputs.build_status }}] PR #${{ steps.pr_info.outputs.pr_number }}: ${{ steps.pr_info.outputs.pr_title }}"
            }],
            "from": {"email": "builds@yourdomain.com"},
            "content": [{
              "type": "text/html",
              "value": "<h2>PR Build Notification</h2><p><strong>Feature:</strong> ${{ steps.pr_info.outputs.pr_title }}</p><p><strong>PR:</strong> #${{ steps.pr_info.outputs.pr_number }}</p><p><strong>Author:</strong> ${{ steps.pr_info.outputs.pr_author }}</p><p><strong>Build Status:</strong> ${{ steps.build.outputs.build_status }}</p><p><strong>Build Time:</strong> ${{ steps.build.outputs.build_time }}</p><p><a href='${{ github.event.pull_request.html_url }}'>View Pull Request</a></p>"
            }]
          }
          EOF
          
          curl -X POST https://api.sendgrid.com/v3/mail/send \
            -H "Authorization: Bearer $SENDGRID_API_KEY" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d @email.json

      - name: Update Deployment Tracker
        if: success()
        uses: actions/github-script@v6
        with:
          script: |
            const fs = require('fs');
            const prTitle = `${{ steps.pr_info.outputs.pr_title }}`;
            const prNumber = ${{ steps.pr_info.outputs.pr_number }};
            
            const deploymentLog = {
              timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
              pr_number: prNumber,
              pr_title: prTitle,
              commit_sha: context.payload.pull_request.head.sha,
              author: `${{ steps.pr_info.outputs.pr_author }}`,
              build_status: `${{ steps.build.outputs.build_status }}`
            };
            
            console.log('Deployment tracked:', JSON.stringify(deploymentLog, null, 2));
            console.log('This information can be used for release notes automation');

      - name: Tag Commit with PR Info
        if: success()
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          
          TAG_NAME="pr-${{ steps.pr_info.outputs.pr_number }}-build-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)"
          TAG_MESSAGE="Build for PR #${{ steps.pr_info.outputs.pr_number }}: ${{ steps.pr_info.outputs.pr_title }}"
          
          echo "Would create tag: $TAG_NAME with message: $TAG_MESSAGE"
          # Uncomment to actually create tags:
          # git tag -a "$TAG_NAME" -m "$TAG_MESSAGE"
          # git push origin "$TAG_NAME"
